nameMeanings.name: Perry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Herry ‘son of Herry’, a variant of the personal name Harry (see Harris ).

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a pear tree (Middle English piri(e), peri(e), puri(e), Old English pirige, pyrige), or a habitational name for someone who owned or came from a place so named. This surname and a number of variants have been established in Ireland since the 17th century.

variants.name: Peery
meanings: Irish: variant of   Perry  , taken to Ireland at the time of thePlantation of Ulster and in the Cromwellian period.

variants.name: Pear
meanings: English: topographic name from residence by a pear tree (Middle English pere, Old English peru, pere). Compare Perry .
English: nickname from Middle English pere, Old French per, peer ‘peer or paragon’, also ‘companion’.

variants.name: Peary
meanings: History: Robert E. Peary (1856–1920) was a U.S.-born explorer, who in 1909 led the first successful expedition to the North Pole. Peary Land in the north of Greenland is named after him, as he explored there in 1892 and 1900.

variants.name: Perrie
meanings: Scottish: variant of Perry 2. Alternatively, perhaps from the Old French personal name Pierre; see Pierce .

variants.name: Harris
meanings: English (southern England and south Wales): from the personal name Harry + genitival -s. This surname is also established in Ireland, taken there principally during the Plantation of Ulster. However, in some cases, particularly in families coming from County Mayo, Harris can be an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEarchadha. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

American shortened and altered form of Greek surnames begining with Cha(r)-, such as Chasandrinos (variant of Kassandrinos, a habitational name from the Kassandra peninsula of Chalkidiki), and various patronymics from the personal name Charalampos (see Charos ). In North America, the surname Harris may possibly also originate from a transferred use of the Greek personal (given) name Charis or Harris (shortened forms of Charalampos) as a surname (i.e. as a replacement of the original surname).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Perryman
meanings: English (Devon): elaborated form of Perry , with Middle English man, probably denoting one who lived near a pear tree; see Pearman .

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.description: First settled by people from Fairfield.
Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.
Date of Statehood: February 6, 1788, the fifth State

historicEvents.title: Brass is Discovered
historicEvents.description: "In 1802, brass was identified in Waterbury, Connecticut. This gave the city the nickname ""The Brass City."" Brass dominated the city and helped to create the city. The motto of the city is Quid Aere Perennius, which means What is more lasting than brass? in Latin."
historicEvents.urlTitle: Connecticut History.Org- Birth of the Brass Valley

historicEvents.description: France sells Louisiana territories to U.S.A.

historicEvents.title: Farmington Canal Opened
historicEvents.description: Farmington Canal spans 2,476 acres, starting from New Haven, Connecticut, and on to Northampton, Massachusetts. The groundbreaking for the canal was in 1825 and opened in 1829.
historicEvents.urlTitle: ConnecticutHistory.org- New England's Grand Ambition:The Farmington Canal

historicEvents.title: The Second Great Awakening
historicEvents.description: Being a second spiritual and religious awakening, like the First Great Awakening, many Churches began to spring up from other denominations. Many people began to rapidly join the Baptist and Methodist congregations. Many converts to these religions believed that the Awakening was the precursor of a new millennial age.

en.lifeSummary: Sarah Perry was born in 1750, in Worcester, Worcester, Massachusetts, United States. She had at least 1 daughter with Johns. She died in 1847, in Connecticut, United States, at the age of 97, and was buried in Easton, Fairfield, Connecticut, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Sarah Perry est née en 1750, à Worcester, Worcester, Massachusetts, États-Unis. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Johns. Elle est décédée en 1847, à Connecticut, États-Unis, à l’âge de 97 ans, et a été enterrée à Easton, Fairfield, Connecticut, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Sarah Perry wurde 1750 in Worcester, Worcester, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten geboren. Sie hatte mit Johns mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1847 im Alter von 97 Jahren in Connecticut, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Easton, Fairfield, Connecticut, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Sarah Perryは1750年にアメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州ウオルセスターウオルセスターで生まれた。彼女はJohnsとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1847年にアメリカ合衆国コネチカット州で97歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ファイルフィールドエアストンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Perry(은)는 1750년 미국 매사추세츠 주 워스스터 워스스터에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Johns(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1847년 미국 코네티컷 주에서 97세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 코네티컷 주 파어필드 이스트에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Sarah Perry родилась в 1750 г. в местности ворсестир, ворсестир, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Johns.
